t: DSDS (Decreased Sexual Desire Screener) — 5-item in women.
Hormonal assessment (morning testosterone in men; when indicated in women).
In SSRI-induced cases, bupropion, mirtazapine, vortioxetine alternatives.
Pharmacotherapy in Women (Premenopausal Hypoactive Sexual Desire Disorder):
Flibanserin (100 mg at night) — FDA 2015 approval; caution with alcohol (modification in BPS);
Bremelanotide (1.75 mg s/c as needed) — FDA 2019 approval; melanocortin agonist.
Transdermal testosterone in low doses (off-label, international consensus Davis S.R. 2019) – careful monitoring.
hypogonadism confirmed → TRT (≥2 low morning measurements + symptoms).
Relationship and communication work is critical.
Clayton A.H. et al. ISSWSH Process of Care. Mayo Clin Proc 2018.
Davis S.R. et al. Global Consensus Position Statement on Testosterone Therapy for Women. J Clin Endocrinol Metab 2019.
Serotonin 1A agonist / 2A antagonist; nightly dose; in premenopausal women.
— Brotto L.A. studies — effective in female sexual dysfunction.
With multimodal approach, most patients improve; chronic form requires ongoing strategy.
Myth 1: "Low desire is always a disorder"
distress critical criterion; low desire without distress normal variation—not pathological.
Myth 2: "Testosterone is a libido enhancer for all women"
Davis 2019 consensus — low-dose off-label only for postmenopausal HSDD; general timing of administration is not an official recommendation.
The mechanisms are entirely different (serotonergic, central nervous system); daily use; the effect is modest (comparison with sildenafil is incorrect).
Myth 4: "Desire dysfunction is only due to psychological causes"
SSRI, OC, antihypertensives, and hormonal changes have a strong effect; biological research is important.
Cochrane and systematic reviews — small or no effect; FDA warnings about hidden components.
WHO. ICD-11. HA02 Hypoactive sexual desire dysfunction. 2024.
Clayton A.H. et al. The International Society for the Study of Women's Sexual Health Process of Care for Management of HSDD in Women. Mayo Clin Proc 2018;93(4):467–487.
Davis S.R. et al. Global Consensus Position Statement on the Use of Testosterone Therapy for Women. J Clin Endocrinol Metab 2019;104(10):4660–4666.
Basson R. The female sexual response: a different model. J Sex Marital Ther 2000;26(1):51–65.
